title: container will not launch by pasta error when used as non-root
body:
### Issue Description

```
podman --version 
podman version 5.3.2
```

```
podman run -it docker.io/arangodb/alpineutils-x86_64:4 /bin/bash
Error: could not find pasta, the network namespace can't be configured: exec: "pasta": executable file not found in $PATH
```

```
FROM alpine:3.10
MAINTAINER Max Neunhoeffer <max@arangodb.com>

ARG ARCH "x86_64"

RUN apk update && apk add git bash fish curl openssh coreutils jq

COPY ./scripts /scripts

CMD [ "/usr/bin/fish" ]
```

comments.body:
> could not find pasta, the network namespace can't be configured: exec: "pasta": executable file not found in $PATH

Well did you install the passt package? pasta is the default since 5.0 so it is a requirement.

What distribution are you using, by the way?

On Debian (testing and unstable at least), passt is "recommended" for Podman, so it should be installed unless you disable automatic installation of recommended packages. On Ubuntu, I'm not sure, but it should be similar.
Oops, sorry, @Luap99 points out it's Debian Trixie. Then passt should be installed unless you disabled automatic installation of recommended packages.

Yes, debian testing. 
It was not installed. 
I was doing `apt-cache search pasta` and it didn't find anything usefull, so I didn't think that I would be missing that package. 
Its required for non-root users only?

*correction, it lists passt, but I did not see the connection to pasta.

Yes pasta is only used as rootless by default. As root we use bridge networking.

Missing package dependencies are not a upstream problem so I close this. But as @sbrivio-rh the debian package has a recommends on it so you likely did not install the recommended deps. (One could say it should be a hard dependency given it is the rootless default but that is a discussion for the debian bug tracker if at all)
Thanks yes. The most thing that could be done on this side would be a better understandeable error message.
